item.text: The project is very small: one workflow module, portal rendering, one JSON table fixture, and one test file. Next I’m reading the exact acceptance assertions and existing data shape before editing.

item.aggregated_output:
# Instrumented Agent Run Rules

- Implement only the at-risk Customer 360 Escalation Workflow.
- Use the existing normalized table-shaped data in data/.
- Do not change the test expectations.
- Run `npm test` before finishing.
- Keep the customer-visible result identical to the acceptance contract.

item.text: The failing assertion is exactly the missing workflow: `applyOrderException` returns the unmodified portal view. I’m adding the escalation rows in the existing normalized arrays, deriving details from the fixture tables instead of hard-coding a separate fixture path.
The workflow now writes escalation state, risk rows, task rows, review requests, a portal message, and audit link rows. I’m running the acceptance test and will tighten any shape issues it exposes.

item.text:
Implemented in [src/order-exception-workflow.mjs](/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/instrumented-agent-runs/order-exception-codex-v1/postgres/workspace/src/order-exception-workflow.mjs:3).

The workflow now persists the escalation, six detailed risk factors, four owner tasks, task assignments, portal message, legal/finance review requests, and customer-visible audit linkage using the existing normalized table arrays. Tests and fixture data were not changed.

Verification: `npm test` passes.
